subject: Chemistry
q:
Number of crystal systems from the following where body centred unit cell can be found, is _____.

Cubic, tetragonal, orthorhombic, hexagonal, rhombohedral, monoclinic, triclinic
question:
Number of crystal systems from the following where body centred unit cell can be found, is _____.

Cubic, tetragonal, orthorhombic, hexagonal, rhombohedral, monoclinic, triclinic
solution:
Bravais Lattice refers to the 14 different 3-dimensional configurations into which atoms can be arranged in crystals. The smallest group of symmetrically aligned atoms which can be repeated in an array to make up the entire crystal is called a unit cell. Crystal systems where body centred unit cell can be found are Cubic, orthorhombic and tetragonal

Hence, correct answer is 3

subject: Chemistry
q:
Which of the following represents the lattice structure of A0.95O containing A2+, A3+ and O2– ions?

A.

B.

C.
question:
Which of the following represents the lattice structure of A0.95O containing A2+, A3+ and O2– ions?

A.

B.

C.
solution:
Applying the electrical neutrality principle in metal deficiency defect.

One A2+ is missing then two A2+ ions are converted into A3+ ion so 0.05 A2+ ion are missing and 0.05×2  A2+ ion are converted in A3+ ion so for each O2- ion total A2+ ion 0.85 and A3+ ion is 0.1.

Hence, A satisfies the condition.

subject: Chemistry
q: In a solid <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>AB</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> atoms are in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ccp</mi></math> arrangement and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> atoms occupy all the octahedral sites. If two atoms from the opposite faces are removed, then the resultant stoichiometry of the compound is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></msub></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></math> is [nearest integer]
question: In a solid <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>AB</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> atoms are in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ccp</mi></math> arrangement and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> atoms occupy all the octahedral sites. If two atoms from the opposite faces are removed, then the resultant stoichiometry of the compound is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></msub></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></math> is [nearest integer]
solution:
A forms ccp structure that means number of A atoms per unit cell →4

B atoms per unit cell →4 (Octahedral voids), as number of octahedral voids will be equal to number of atoms per unit cell.

A removed =2×12=1

A left =4-1=3

Formula becomes A3B4

x=3.

subject: Chemistry
q: An element crystallises in a face–centred cubic (fcc) unit cell with cell edge a. The distance between the centres of two nearest octahedral voids in the crystal lattice is:
question: An element crystallises in a face–centred cubic (fcc) unit cell with cell edge a. The distance between the centres of two nearest octahedral voids in the crystal lattice is:
solution:
In FCC octahedral voids are present at the edge centers and body center.

Minimum distance between centers of two octahedral voids

=x=a22+a22
=a24+a24=a2

subject: Chemistry
q: Among the following which is the best description of water in the solid phase?
question: Among the following which is the best description of water in the solid phase?
solution: Molecular solid is the best description of water in the solid phase. For example ice is hydrogen bonded molecular solid.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I : Frenkel defects are vacancy as well as interstitial defects.
Statement II : Frenkel defect leads to colour in ionic solids due to presence of F-centres.

Choose the most appropriate answer for the statements from the options given below:
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I : Frenkel defects are vacancy as well as interstitial defects.
Statement II : Frenkel defect leads to colour in ionic solids due to presence of F-centres.

Choose the most appropriate answer for the statements from the options given below:
options: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
solution: A Frenkel defect is another form of a point defect which is created when an atom or cation leaves its original place in the lattice structure to create a vacancy while occupying another interstitial position within the solid crystal. Frenkel defect is dislocation defect, it is not interstitial defect, and it is not responsible for colour of solid.

This solid is most likely to be a / an :
question:

This solid is most likely to be a / an :
solution: Covalent or network solid have very high melting point, and they are insulators in their solid and molten form.

q: Percentage of free space in cubic close packed structure and in body centred packed structure are respectively
question: Percentage of free space in cubic close packed structure and in body centred packed structure are respectively
solution: Packing fraction of cubic close packing and body centred packing are $0.74$ and $0.68$ respectively.

subject: Chemistry
q:
The radius of the largest sphere which fits properly at the centre of the edge of a body centred cubic unit cell is :

(Edge length is represented by 'a')
question:
The radius of the largest sphere which fits properly at the centre of the edge of a body centred cubic unit cell is :

(Edge length is represented by 'a')
solution:
For bcc

$$

\Rightarrow \mathrm{R}=\frac{\sqrt{3} \mathrm{a}}{4}

$$

$$

\therefore \text { Empty space at edge }=a-2 R=a-\frac{\sqrt{3} a}{2}

$$

$=$ diameter of sphere.

$$

\therefore \quad \mathrm{r}_{\text {sphere }}=\frac{a-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \mathrm{a}}{2}=\left(\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{4}\right) \mathrm{a}=0.067 \mathrm{a}

$$

subject: Chemistry
q: In a face centered cubic lattice atoms A are at the corner points and atoms B at the face centered points. If atom B is missing from one of the face centered points, the formula of the ionic compound is :
question: In a face centered cubic lattice atoms A are at the corner points and atoms B at the face centered points. If atom B is missing from one of the face centered points, the formula of the ionic compound is :
solution:
A atoms at corners  = 8 × 1 8 =  A (one atom)

B atom is missing at One face centre then will present at 5 faces

At face centre atom contribute 1/2 part

Number of B atom at Face centre = 5× 1 2 (B atom)

So formula of compound = A2B5

q: Which one of the following statements about packing in solids is incorrect ?
question: Which one of the following statements about packing in solids is incorrect ?
options: Coordination number in bcc mode of packing is 8 .
Coordination number in hep mode of packing is 12 .
Void space in hcp mode of packing is $32 \%$.
Void space in ccp mode of packing is $26 \%$.
solution: The $h c p$ arrangement of atoms occupies $74 \%$ of the available space and thus has $26 \%$ vacant space.

subject: Chemistry
q: Among the following the incorrect statement is
question: Among the following the incorrect statement is
options: Density of crystals remains unaffected due to Frenkel defect.
In BCC unit cell the void space is $32 \%$.
Density of crystals decreases due to Schottky defect.
Electrical conductivity of semiconductors and metals increases with increase in temperature.
solution: The electrical resistance of metals depends upon temperature. Electrical resistance decreases with decrease in temperature and becomes zero near the absolute temperature. Material in this state is said to possess super conductivity.

subject: Chemistry
q: An element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> crystallises in a body centred cubic unit cell with a cell edge of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>300</mn><mi>pm</mi></math>. The density of the element is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The number of atoms present in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>180</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of the element is____<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>23</mn></msup></math>. (Nearest integer)
question: An element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> crystallises in a body centred cubic unit cell with a cell edge of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>300</mn><mi>pm</mi></math>. The density of the element is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The number of atoms present in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>180</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of the element is____<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>23</mn></msup></math>. (Nearest integer)
solution:
M is body centred cubic, ∴ Z=2

Let mass of 1 atom of M is A

Edge length =300pm

Density =6 g/cm3

∴ 6 g/cm3=Z×A300×10-103=2×A27×10-24

A=81×10-24 g

Atoms of M=Total massMass of one atom = 18081×10-24 = 22.22×1023≅22×1023

subject: Chemistry
q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Ga</mi></math> (atomic mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>70</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi></math>) crystallizes in a hexagonal close packed structure. The total number of voids in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>581</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Ga</mi></math> is ________ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>21</mn></msup></math>. (Round off to the Nearest Integer).
question: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Ga</mi></math> (atomic mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>70</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi></math>) crystallizes in a hexagonal close packed structure. The total number of voids in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>581</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Ga</mi></math> is ________ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>21</mn></msup></math>. (Round off to the Nearest Integer).
solution:
HCP structure : Per atom, there will be one octahedral void (OV) and two tetrahedral voids (TV).

Therefore, total three voids per atom are present in HCP structure.

→ Therefore total no. of atoms of Ga will be-

= Mass  Molar Mass ×NA=0.581 g70 g/mol×6.023×1023

→ Now, total Number of voids =3 × total no. of atoms 

=3×0.58170×6.023×1023=14.99×1021

≃15×1021

subject: Chemistry
q: A compound of formula <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> has the HCP lattice. Which atom forms the HCP lattice and what fraction of the tetrahedral voids are occupied by the other atoms?
question: A compound of formula <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> has the HCP lattice. Which atom forms the HCP lattice and what fraction of the tetrahedral voids are occupied by the other atoms?
solution:
Atom B forms HCP as anions are larger in size.

From the formula A2B3,

Number of B atoms=3,

Then, number of tetrahedral voids =2×Tetrahedral voids=2×3=6.

As 'A' is occupying tetrahedral voids,

  1 3  of tetrahedral voids=13×6=2

Therefore, formula of the compound=A2B3

subject: Chemistry
q: In a compound atoms of element $Y$ from ccp lattice and those of element $X$ occupy $2 / 3^{\text {rd }}$ of tetrahedral voids. The formula of the compound will be
question: In a compound atoms of element $Y$ from ccp lattice and those of element $X$ occupy $2 / 3^{\text {rd }}$ of tetrahedral voids. The formula of the compound will be
solution:
No. of atoms of $Y=4$
No. of atoms of $X=\frac{2}{3} \times 8$
Formula of compound will be $X_4 Y_3$

subject: Chemistry
q: How many unit cells are present in a cubeshaped ideal crystal of $\mathrm{NaCl}$ of mass $1.00 \mathrm{~g}$ ? [Atomic masses: $\mathrm{Na}=23, \mathrm{Cl}=35.5$ ]
question: How many unit cells are present in a cubeshaped ideal crystal of $\mathrm{NaCl}$ of mass $1.00 \mathrm{~g}$ ? [Atomic masses: $\mathrm{Na}=23, \mathrm{Cl}=35.5$ ]
solution:
Number of formulas in cube shaped crystals $=\frac{1.0}{58.5} \times 6.02 \times 10^{23}$ since in $\mathrm{NaCl}$ type of structure 4 formula units form a cell
$\therefore$ unit cells $=\frac{1.0 \times 6.02 \times 10^{23}}{58.5 \times 4}=2.57 \times 10^{21}$ unit cells.

subject: Chemistry
q: A crystal is made up of metal ions <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>'</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>'</mo></math> and oxide ions. Oxide ions form a ccp lattice structure. The cation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>'</mo></math> occupies <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of octahedral voids and the cation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>'</mo></math> occupies <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mo>%</mo></math> of tetrahedral voids of oxide lattice. The oxidation numbers of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>'</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>'</mo></math> are respectively :
question: A crystal is made up of metal ions <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>'</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>'</mo></math> and oxide ions. Oxide ions form a ccp lattice structure. The cation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>'</mo></math> occupies <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of octahedral voids and the cation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>'</mo></math> occupies <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mo>%</mo></math> of tetrahedral voids of oxide lattice. The oxidation numbers of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>'</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>'</mo></math> are respectively :
solution:
In the ccp latice of oxide ions effective number of O-2ions =8×18+6×12=4

In the ccp latice,

No. of octahedral voids =4

No. of tetrahedrla voids =8

Given M1 atoms occupies 50% of octahedral voids and M2 atoms occupies 12.5 of tetrahederal voids

No. of M1 metal atoms =4×50100=2

No. of M2 metal atoms =8×12.5100=1

∴ Formula of the compound =M12M2O4

∴ Oxidation states of metals M1 & M2 respectively are +2 and +4

subject: Chemistry
q:
The distance between Na+ and Cl- ions in solid NaCl of density 43.1 gcm-3 is .....×10-10 m. (Nearest Integer)
(Given : NA=6.02×1023 mol-1)
question:
The distance between Na+ and Cl- ions in solid NaCl of density 43.1 gcm-3 is .....×10-10 m. (Nearest Integer)
(Given : NA=6.02×1023 mol-1)
solution:
For NaCl Z=4 and M=58.5 gram

d=Z×MNA×Volume

43.1=4×58.56.02×1023×a3

a3=4×58.56.02×43.1×10-23

=0.9×10-23

=9×10-24

a=2.08×10-8 cm

dNa++Cl-=a2=2.08×10-102 m ≃1×10-10 m

question:

subject: Chemistry
q:
Iron oxide FeO, crystallises in a cubic lattice with a unit cell edge length of 5.0Å. If density of the FeO in the crystal is 4.0 g cm-3, then the number of FeO units present per unit cell is _____ (Nearest integer)

Given : Molar mass of Fe and O is 56 and 16 g mol-1 respectively.
NA=6.0×1023 mol-1
question:
Iron oxide FeO, crystallises in a cubic lattice with a unit cell edge length of 5.0Å. If density of the FeO in the crystal is 4.0 g cm-3, then the number of FeO units present per unit cell is _____ (Nearest integer)

Given : Molar mass of Fe and O is 56 and 16 g mol-1 respectively.
NA=6.0×1023 mol-1
solution:
The density of the crystal can be calculated as follows,

d=Z×MN0×a3

d=densityZ=effective number of atomsM=Molar massN0=Avogadro's numbera=Edge length

4=Z×726×1023×125×10-24

Z=4.166≃4

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following exists as covalent crystals in the solid state ?
question: Which of the following exists as covalent crystals in the solid state ?
solution:
Silicon is covalent or network solid.

Each Si atom is in sp3 hybridisation.

Sulphur (S8)  –  molecular solid.

Phosphorus (P4)  – molecular solid.

Iodine (I2)   –  molecular solid.

subject: Chemistry
q: A compound is formed by two elements X and Y. The element Y forms cubic close packed arrangement and those of element X occupy one third of the tetrahedral voids. What is the formula of the compound?
question: A compound is formed by two elements X and Y. The element Y forms cubic close packed arrangement and those of element X occupy one third of the tetrahedral voids. What is the formula of the compound?
solution:
Atoms of Element Y, are present in cubic close packing. In cubic close packing the number of atoms per unit cell is 4.

So, the effective number of element Y, per unit cell is 4

Element X, are present at one third of the tetrahedral voids.

Number of tetrahedral void =Twice the number of atoms in unit cell

Number of atoms of Element X are present unit cell =13×8=83

X : Y83 : 42   : 3

Formula of the compound =X2Y3

subject: Chemistry
q:
A metal M forms hexagonal close-packed structure. The total number of voids in 0.02 mol of it is _____ ×1021 (Nearest integer)

(Given NA=6.02×1023)
question:
A metal M forms hexagonal close-packed structure. The total number of voids in 0.02 mol of it is _____ ×1021 (Nearest integer)

(Given NA=6.02×1023)
solution:
From the diagram of voids in HCP:

There are 6 octahedral voids and 12 tetrahedral voids in a HCP unit cell.
One unit cell of HCP contains =18 voids

Thus, No. of voids in 0.02 mol of hcp

=186×6.02×1023×0.02

≈3.6×1022

≈36×1021

subject: Chemistry
q: Consider the bcc unit cells of the solids 1 and 2 with the position of atoms as shown below. The radius of atom B  is twice that of atom A. The unit cell edge length is 50 % more in solid 2 than in 1 . What is the approximate packing efficiency in solid 2 ?
question: Consider the bcc unit cells of the solids 1 and 2 with the position of atoms as shown below. The radius of atom B  is twice that of atom A. The unit cell edge length is 50 % more in solid 2 than in 1 . What is the approximate packing efficiency in solid 2 ?
solution:
If radius of A is r radius of B will be 2r . Atoms in the body diagonal touch.

Therefore:
r+2r+2r+r=3 a

6 r=3 a or a=6r3

Packing Efficiency
=1×43πr3+1×43π(2r)3a3=43πr3×9(6r/3)3
=4×9×π×r3×333×216×r3=90.5 %

subject: Chemistry
q: <math> <mrow> <mtext>CsCl</mtext></mrow> </math> crystallises in body centred cubic lattice. If 'a' is its edge length then which of the following expressions is correct?
question: <math> <mrow> <mtext>CsCl</mtext></mrow> </math> crystallises in body centred cubic lattice. If 'a' is its edge length then which of the following expressions is correct?
solution:
8 chloride ions at 8 corners Cs + cation at body centre along body diagonal there will be one Cs + cation and 2  Cl − ions at ends of body diagonal.
2 r Cs + + 2 r Cl - = 3 a
a is edge length

q:
A copper complex crystallising in a CCP lattice with a cell edge of 0.4518 nm has been revealed by employing X-ray diffraction studies. The density of a copper complex is found to be 7.62 g cm-3. The molar mass of copper complex is       gmol-1
(Nearest integer): [ Given :NA=6.022×1023 mol-1]
question:
A copper complex crystallising in a CCP lattice with a cell edge of 0.4518 nm has been revealed by employing X-ray diffraction studies. The density of a copper complex is found to be 7.62 g cm-3. The molar mass of copper complex is       gmol-1
(Nearest integer): [ Given :NA=6.022×1023 mol-1]
solution:
dgmcc=4×MNA(a cm)3
7.62=4×M/6.022×10230.4518×10-7 cm3⇒M=105.8 g/mol

subject: Chemistry
q: In a binary compound, atoms of element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> form a hcp structure and those of element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> occupy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>/</mo><mn>3</mn></math> of the tetrahedral voids of the hcp structure. The formula of the binary compound is:
question: In a binary compound, atoms of element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> form a hcp structure and those of element <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> occupy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>/</mo><mn>3</mn></math> of the tetrahedral voids of the hcp structure. The formula of the binary compound is:
solution:
In HCP unit cell number of effective number of atoms is 6,

so  A=6.

Number of tetrahedral voids

                                  = 2 x number of atoms forming the lattice 

                                  =2 X 6

M12×23 A6
M8 A6
M4 A3

subject: Chemistry
q: The oxide that shows magnetic property is:
question: The oxide that shows magnetic property is:
solution: Mn3O4 shows magnetic properties.
